The '''Nebulon-B2 frigate''', sometimes referred to as the '''modified frigate''', was a relative of the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B escort frigate]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:Manus_ferre.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The distinctive bank of sublight engines on a Nebulon-B2]]
Though it shared a similar profile with its predecessor, the Nebulon-B2 differed in some key ways. The forward pod split into an inverted-V shape which allowed for greater surface area to mount weaponry; furthermore the connecting spar was heavier, rendering it less vulnerable to being [[Provocateur|split open by heavy fire]], and the ship was about 50 meters shorter overall.

Sublight speed was 17% faster, and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] strength and [[Hull/Legends|hull]] durability were both improved. The Nebulon-B2 was also armed with a [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] launcher, which was not normally mounted on its predecessor.

==Behind the scenes==
Like many other craft pioneered by [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], the Nebulon-B2 has not appeared in any official material outside of the games.

The Nebulon-B2 may seem somewhat under-gunned for a ship that is supposed to be superior to its predecessor and is described in-game as being "exceptionally well armed for its size." This is presumed to be due to the necessities of game mechanics, since a player in a single [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] would have little chance of defeating a more realistically armed ship. In ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'', the source of the statistics for this ship, the original Nebulon-B is armed only with four [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]].

The Nebulon-B2 has gone through numerous changes between game versions. While its shielding and hull strength was equal to that of a [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught'']] in ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter]]: Balance of Power'', its statistics were changed by ''X-wing Alliance'', where it had weaker shielding and armor than its predecessor, and was also shorter.

A wrecked Nebulon-B2 frigate can be seen during the [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] Junkyard mission in the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|''Shadows of the Empire'' video game]].
